name: Colorful 2-bedroom house
subtitle: Colorful house in the hills of Tenerife
tagline: Your cosy mountain retreat in Valleseco
welcomeMessage: Welcome to my colourful house in the hills of Tenerife! Nestled in the charming village of Valleseco, it is full of character and surrounded by nature. I hope you love it as much as I do. Reach out anytime if you need anything at all.

address.display: Calle Manuel Ravina 69, Valleseco, Santa Cruz de Tenerife
address.nearestLandmark: Near Punto y Coma Minimarket, Valleseco village

checkin.keyLocation: Look for the blue door on the upper part of the house — the keybox is right next to it.
steps: Park at the recommended parking area (there is always space, and you avoid almost any stairs)
From the parking, walk toward the end of the street, then take the stairs down on the left side
At the next street below, turn right — this is the street of the house
Walk straight, cross the small garden with cacti and plants, and continue between the houses
After about 1 minute, on the left you will see a white house with a blue door
It is the upper part of the house — take the stairs next to the house to reach the entrance
Find the blue door with the keybox next to it. Code: 1214
checkin.notes: If you have any trouble finding me, call or WhatsApp anytime, I am happy to guide you step by step. Note: Google Maps is not 100% accurate for the last stretch, so follow the photo guide!

gettingHere.byCar: The best is to park at the recommended spot (link below). There are always spaces available and you won't have to climb almost any stairs. Then follow the walking directions in the photo guide. Google Maps is not 100% accurate for the last stretch, so please use the photo guide!
gettingHere.parking: There are always parking spaces available and it is a short flat walk to the house entrance.
gettingHere.publicTransport: There are various buses from Santa Cruz de Tenerife (e.g. Nr. 909, 910, 916). Make sure to get off at Valleseco (it's a small village with just one stop). Once you get off, find the Punto y Coma Minimarket and take the stairs on the left about 20 metres after passing it. It's a 5-10 minute uphill walk to the house. Google Maps is not fully accurate for the last stretch, so please follow the photo guide!

directionPhotos.caption: Park up here. There are always spaces available and you won't have to climb almost any stairs.
Starting from the parking, move in this direction and walk until the street ends, then go down the stairs on the left side.
Go down to the next street below and turn right. This is the street of the house.
Walk straight and cross the garden with cacti and other plants.
After 1 min, on the left-hand side, you will see a white-coloured house with a blue door.
Getting closer. Your apartment is in the upper part of the house.
It's the upper part of the house. Take the stairs right next to the house to reach the entrance. Look for the blue door with the key box next to it. Code: 1214

gettingHere.taxi: Taxis are available from Tenerife North (TFN) and Tenerife South (TFS) airports. Agree on the fare before departing or ensure the meter is running. Ask the driver to drop you at the parking spot linked below, then follow the By Car step-by-step photo guide from photo 1.

directionPhotosBus.caption: Find the Punto y Coma Minimarket. After passing it, take the stairs on the left to go up.
Find the stairs on the left, about 20 metres after passing the minimarket.
After 5-10 minutes of climbing (depending on your ambitions!), you will see the house.
It's the upper part of the house. Take the stairs right next to the house to reach the entrance. Look for the blue door with the key box next to it. Code: 1214

houseRules: Please smoke outside only. The outdoor areas are all yours, but kindly keep the inside of the house smoke-free. Thank you!
Please keep it peaceful. Quiet hours are 11pm to 8am. No parties or events
Please treat the apartment as your own, and let me know right away if anything gets damaged. I understand accidents happen

appliances.name: Electricity
appliances.instructions: The house's electrical system is a little old, so it can't handle several high-power appliances running at once: for example a hair dryer plus the oven plus the stove. To be on the safe side, please use just one of these at a time. If the electricity ever cuts out, don't worry at all, just let me know as soon as you can and I'll help sort it out.

appliances.name: Kitchen
appliances.instructions: You have a kitchen area equipped with an electric stove, a mini oven, a fridge as well as the normal kitchen tools you might need. ⚠️ Important: as it's always summer in Tenerife, bugs and insects never sleep. Please make sure to remove all leftover food and throw it in the bin right away, and I also recommend cleaning the dishes right after eating to avoid flies and other insects showing up in the kitchen.

appliances.name: Washing Machine
appliances.instructions: You'll find the washing machine in the kitchen, please feel free to use it. The fast programme called "Rapido" is usually all you need. If you have any questions, just let me know!

appliances.name: Drinking Water
appliances.instructions: Tap water in Tenerife is said to be safe and clean, though most people prefer bottled water, which you'll find at any supermarket or kiosk. The cheaper and more practical option is to grab a 5L, 8L or larger gallon.

appliances.name: Bathroom
appliances.instructions: You'll find fresh towels, a hair dryer, and soap and shampoo all ready for you. If anything else is missing, just let me know and I'll sort it out. ⚠️ The toilet flush can be a little temperamental now and then: if the water doesn't stop running, simply press the button one more time and it will settle.

appliances.name: The Magical Terrace
appliances.instructions: This is the exact same terrace you saw in the photos! Because of Tenerife's wonderfully unpredictable micro climate (hello rain, wind and sunshine, sometimes all in one day! 🌧️💨☀️), I can't leave the pillows, covers and rugs outside, so you'll first find it looking a little bare with a covered BBQ. The good news: everything you need to make it cosy and beautiful is waiting for you, either in the beige closet on the terrace or inside the apartment. Feel free to set it up however you like and simply enjoy! 🏝️🪴

appliances.name: BBQ
appliances.instructions: Up on the terrace you'll find the BBQ, which runs on charcoal. I don't usually keep charcoal in the house unless previous guests have left some, but you can pick up everything you need at any supermarket or local mini market for a lovely grill afternoon or evening. 🙂 ⚠️ Please remember to give the BBQ a good clean after using it, and make sure it's fully out. Thank you so much! 🙏

appliances.name: Plants
appliances.instructions: If you're staying a few days, the plants would love a little water every 3-4 days. They'll appreciate it!

appliances.name: Mari, the Lovely Neighbour
appliances.instructions: My right hand and greatest help is the lovely Mari, who lives right next door. If anything comes up at all (say you need an extra towel), she's always happy to help. She might even pop by with some homemade sweets if she has the time! Mari speaks only Spanish, but don't worry if you don't, somehow it always works out beautifully. 🙂

items.name: Playa de Valleseco / Los Charcos de Valleseco
items.tip: Closest swim, quick dip, local feel. Closest option: a small urban gravel beach with calm waters and easy access. Practical rather than pretty, but great for a quick dip.

items.name: Playa de Las Teresitas
items.tip: Best easy beach day, families, swimming. Golden sand, full facilities, restaurants, and calm water protected by a breakwater. The safest top recommendation from this location.

items.name: Playa de Las Gaviotas
items.tip: Black sand, quieter beach, hidden gem. A wilder black sand beach close to Las Teresitas. Good when you want something less polished and less crowded, but the sea can be rougher.

items.name: Playa de Igueste de San Andrés
items.tip: Quiet village, scenic coast, hidden gem. A beautiful local escape beyond San Andrés. Better for a scenic coastal visit than a classic sandy beach day.

items.name: Playa de Antequera
items.distance: Accessible by boat or long hike
items.tip: Remote beach, adventure, wild nature. One of the true hidden gems of Anaga. Not practical for a casual visit, but absolutely amazing for adventurous guests.

items.name: Playa del Roque de las Bodegas
items.tip: Scenic drive, surf, restaurants nearby. A great Anaga day trip stop: beautiful black sand and dramatic mountain scenery, with restaurants close by. Better for scenery and food than calm swimming.

items.name: Playa de Almáciga
items.tip: Wild black sand, surfers, nature. Beautiful and dramatic, but not the easiest swimming beach. Best for guests who love wild coastlines and don't expect sunbeds.

items.name: Playa de Benijo
items.tip: Sunset, photography, wild Anaga scenery. One of the most beautiful beaches in Tenerife for photos and sunsets: wild, black sand, with strong ocean conditions. Check sea conditions before going.

items.name: Bajamar Natural Pools
items.tip: Safe sea pools, families, wave watching. Not a beach but very worth it. Great when the north coast is dramatic and you want a safer ocean experience.

items.name: Punta del Hidalgo Natural Pools
items.tip: Natural pools, coastal walk, sunset. An excellent coastal day trip: more about swimming pools, lava coast, and stunning views than sand.

items.name: Playa de Mesa del Mar
items.tip: Black sand, local beach, north coast vibe. A nice alternative on the north coast, good for guests exploring Tacoronte or the wine area.

items.name: Playa de El Bollullo
items.tip: Beautiful black sand, north coast classic. One of the prettiest black sand beaches in northern Tenerife. Worth it if you are already visiting La Orotava or Puerto de la Cruz.

items.name: Playa Jardín, Puerto de la Cruz
items.tip: Puerto de la Cruz day trip. Normally famous and scenic, but water quality closures have affected Playa Jardín since 2024. Check current bathing status before going.

items.name: Playa de Valleseco coastal walk
items.tip: Closest sea walk, local feel, quick stroll. The easiest coastal walk from the house. Practical rather than polished, but very local and good for a quick sea view.

items.name: Rambla de Santa Cruz
items.distance: 2-3 km, car, taxi, bus, or long walk
items.tip: City walk, trees, cafes, local life. A pleasant urban walk through one of the main green boulevards of Santa Cruz. Lovely for a relaxed morning or late afternoon.

items.name: Parque García Sanabria
items.distance: 3-4 km, car, taxi, bus, or long walk
items.tip: Shaded walk, plants, fountains, sculptures. One of the best easy scenic walks in the city, perfect when you want greenery without leaving Santa Cruz.

items.name: Plaza de España & Castillo de San Cristóbal
items.distance: 3.5-4.5 km, car, taxi, bus, or walk
items.tip: City history, central square, easy sightseeing. A great starting point for exploring central Santa Cruz. The underground interpretation centre preserves remains of the old castle, capital of Tenerife since 1833.

items.name: Iglesia de Nuestra Señora de la Concepción & La Noria
items.distance: 4-5 km, car, taxi, bus, or walk
items.tip: Old streets, church, bars, local evening walk. Lovely for a historical city stroll. The church and nearby La Noria streets are among the most atmospheric parts of old Santa Cruz.

items.name: Mercado Nuestra Señora de África
items.tip: Local market, food, architecture, morning walk. Not a classic monument, but a great cultural stop. Best in the morning for local produce, snacks, and atmosphere.

items.name: Auditorio de Tenerife
items.tip: Modern architecture, photos, sea walk. One of Santa Cruz's most iconic modern landmarks, designed by Santiago Calatrava. Stunning for photos along the seafront.

items.name: Palmetum of Santa Cruz
items.tip: Botanical walk, sea views, unusual garden. A very good scenic walk inside the city. A 12 hectare botanical garden specialising in palm trees.

items.name: San Andrés village
items.tip: Village feel, seafood, Las Teresitas combo. Small, simple, and local. Great to combine with Las Teresitas beach or a drive into Anaga.

items.name: Mirador de Las Teresitas
items.tip: Best beach viewpoint, photos, scenic stop. One of the easiest 'wow view' stops from the house. Best combined with Las Teresitas or San Andrés.

items.name: San Cristóbal de La Laguna Historic Centre
items.distance: 13-16 km, car, taxi, tram, or bus
items.tip: UNESCO town, colonial streets, architecture. One of the best cultural excursions from Santa Cruz. La Laguna's historic centre has been a UNESCO World Heritage Site since 1999.

items.name: Anaga Rural Park scenic drive
items.distance: 20-35 km depending on stop, car or tour
items.tip: Mountains, laurel forest, viewpoints, villages. The best nature and scenery excursion from this location. Anaga is a protected rural park and, since 2015, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve.

items.name: Cruz del Carmen Visitor Centre, Anaga
items.tip: Forest walks, Anaga information, viewpoints. The best starting point for Anaga forest walks, with information and advice for routes in the Rural Park.

items.name: Taganana village
items.tip: Historic village, Anaga scenery, mountain road. A beautiful old village in Anaga with dramatic landscapes. Great for a scenic drive plus a more traditional feel.

items.name: Roque de las Bodegas scenic coast
items.tip: Ocean views, black sand, seafood lunch. More scenic than historical, but excellent for an Anaga day trip. Combine with Taganana, Almáciga, and Benijo.

items.name: Benijo viewpoint and coastal walk
items.tip: Sunset, wild coast, photography. One of the most dramatic scenic spots in Tenerife. Better for views and photos than easy swimming.

items.name: Playa de Valleseco / Los Charcos de Valleseco
items.tip: Closest sea spot, quick dip, local feel. Very close and practical. More local than postcard beautiful, but useful for a quick ocean stop.

items.name: Rambla de Santa Cruz
items.distance: 2-3 km, bus, taxi, car, or long walk
items.tip: Urban walk, cafes, local life. A pleasant city walk, good if you want something easy without leaving Santa Cruz.

items.name: Parque García Sanabria
items.distance: 3-4 km, bus, taxi, car, or long walk
items.tip: Green walk, shade, fountains, sculptures. One of the nicest easy walks in Santa Cruz, especially when the weather is warm.

items.name: Plaza de España & city centre
items.distance: 3.5-4.5 km, bus, taxi, car, or walk
items.tip: City sightseeing, shopping, cafes. A good starting point for a Santa Cruz city walk. Easy to combine with the old streets, harbour area, and the Mercado.

items.name: Castillo de San Cristóbal Interpretation Centre
items.distance: 3.5-4.5 km, bus, taxi, car, or walk
items.tip: Short history stop, city centre. Small but interesting if you are already visiting Plaza de España. Best as a quick add-on rather than a standalone trip.

items.name: Mercado Nuestra Señora de África
items.tip: Local food, market, morning visit. A great cultural food stop. Best in the morning for fruit, coffee, local products, and atmosphere.

items.name: Auditorio de Tenerife
items.tip: Architecture, photos, sea walk. One of Santa Cruz's most iconic modern landmarks, designed by Santiago Calatrava. Good to combine with the Palmetum and waterfront.

items.name: Palmetum of Santa Cruz
items.tip: Botanical garden, sea views, relaxed walk. A 12 hectare botanical garden specialising in palm trees, one of the best quiet attractions inside Santa Cruz.

items.name: San Andrés village
items.tip: Seafood, village feel, Las Teresitas combo. A small local village, best combined with Las Teresitas beach and the Mirador.

items.name: Playa de Las Teresitas
items.tip: Easy beach day, families, swimming. The easiest beach excursion from this location. Golden sand, calm water, facilities, and restaurants nearby.

items.name: Mirador de Las Teresitas
items.tip: Viewpoint, photos, beach panorama. One of the easiest wow views from the house. Best before or after Las Teresitas.

items.name: San Cristóbal de La Laguna Historic Centre
items.distance: 13-16 km, car, taxi, tram, or bus
items.tip: UNESCO town, cafes, architecture. One of the best cultural excursions from Santa Cruz. Great for a half day trip with lunch or coffee.

items.name: Bajamar Natural Pools
items.tip: Ocean pools, families, wave watching. A great alternative to beach swimming, especially when you want ocean water in a more protected spot.

items.name: Anaga Rural Park scenic drive
items.distance: 20-35 km depending on stop, car or tour
items.tip: Mountains, viewpoints, villages, laurel forest. The best nature excursion from this location. Anaga is a protected rural park and a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve.

items.name: Taganana, Roque de las Bodegas & Benijo
items.tip: Dramatic coast, villages, lunch, photos. One of the best day trips from Santa Cruz: mountain roads, black sand beaches, ocean views, and traditional food.

items.name: Cruz del Carmen Visitor Centre
items.tip: Forest walks, viewpoints, Anaga info. The best starting point for easy Anaga forest walks and route advice.

items.name: Punta del Hidalgo
items.tip: Coastal walk, natural pools, sunset. Very good for a relaxed north coast excursion. Combine with Bajamar or La Laguna.

items.name: Loro Parque, Puerto de la Cruz
items.tip: Families, full day attraction. One of Tenerife's biggest tourist attractions. Best for families or guests who enjoy animal parks.

items.name: Puerto de la Cruz & Lago Martiánez
items.tip: City day trip, sea pools, restaurants. A classic north Tenerife day trip. Lago Martiánez is a seawater pool complex designed by César Manrique.

items.name: La Orotava Historic Centre
items.tip: Traditional architecture, old town, views. A beautiful historic town, good to combine with Puerto de la Cruz or El Bollullo beach.

items.name: Teide National Park
items.distance: 60-75 km depending on route, car or organised tour
items.tip: Volcano landscapes, viewpoints, full day trip. The biggest must-do excursion in Tenerife. Includes Mount Teide, Spain's highest peak at 3,718 m, and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

items.name: Masca Village
items.tip: Mountain village, dramatic road, photos. Beautiful but a long drive from Santa Cruz. Best for a full island day trip, ideally with Teide or the Buenavista area.

items.name: Siam Park, Costa Adeje
items.tip: Waterpark, families, thrill rides. Far from this location, but one of Tenerife's most famous attractions. Better as a full day plan, with a typical visit of around 4-6 hours.

items.name: Valleseco coastal walk
items.tip: Easy | Closest sea walk, quick local stroll. Best no-hassle option from the house. More urban and local than wild nature, but useful for a quick sea view.

items.name: Rambla de Santa Cruz walk
items.distance: 2-3 km, bus, taxi, car, or long walk
items.tip: Easy | City walk, shade, cafes. A relaxed urban walk, good when you don't feel like a proper hike.

items.name: Parque García Sanabria
items.distance: 3-4 km, bus, taxi, car, or long walk
items.tip: Easy | Green walk, shade, sculptures. Best easy green walk inside Santa Cruz. Lovely in the morning or late afternoon.

items.name: Palmetum of Santa Cruz
items.tip: Easy | Botanical walk, sea views, plants. A very good scenic walk without leaving the city. A 12 hectare botanical garden focused on palm trees.

items.name: Mirador de Las Teresitas viewpoint walk
items.tip: Easy | Photos, beach panorama, short walk. Not a proper hike, but one of the easiest scenic viewpoints from the house. Combine with Las Teresitas or San Andrés.

items.name: La Laguna to Cruz del Carmen / Las Mercedes forest
items.tip: Easy to moderate | Forest, cooler air, local escape. Good option when you want greenery without driving deep into Anaga. Best on hot days or when the coast feels too exposed.

items.name: Bajamar to Punta del Hidalgo coastal walk
items.tip: Easy to moderate | Natural pools, ocean views, sunset. A great north coast walk with swimming pool stops. Better for coastal scenery than forest hiking.

items.name: Cruz del Carmen: Sendero de los Sentidos
items.tip: Easy | Laurel forest, families, short nature walk. One of the best beginner-friendly Anaga walks: a 1 km circular mostly flat trail, suitable for children and adults alike.

items.name: Cruz del Carmen Visitor Centre forest walks
items.tip: Easy to moderate | Anaga forest, route advice, viewpoints. The best starting point for Anaga forest walks. Great for guests who want guidance before choosing a trail.

items.name: Taganana to Roque de las Bodegas coastal walk
items.tip: Easy to moderate | Dramatic coast, village walk, lunch. More scenic walk than classic hike. Great with lunch near Roque de las Bodegas and photo stops toward Almáciga or Benijo.

items.name: Pico del Inglés viewpoint and forest area
items.tip: Easy to moderate | Anaga views, photos, misty forest. A great scenic stop in Anaga. Best combined with Cruz del Carmen and the laurel forest walks.

items.name: El Pijaral 'Enchanted Forest', Anaga
items.tip: Moderate | Magical laurel forest, mossy Anaga landscape. One of the most special forest hikes in Anaga: note that a permit is required. Book in advance through the official Tenerife permits system.

items.name: Benijo coastal viewpoint walk
items.tip: Moderate | Sunset, wild coastline, photos. Excellent for dramatic coastal scenery. Steps and uneven paths, so better with proper shoes. Avoid risky swimming when the sea is rough.

items.name: Afur to Playa de Tamadite
items.tip: Moderate | Ravine walk, wild beach, adventure. A beautiful Anaga hike through a ravine to a remote beach. Swimming at Tamadite can be dangerous, so treat it as a hiking destination rather than a beach day.

items.name: PR TF 08 Afur to Taganana loop
items.tip: Hard | Serious hikers, views, Anaga villages. A demanding trail with steep sections, descending to Tamadite before a steep climb toward Taganana. Not for the faint-hearted, but spectacular.

items.name: Roques de García loop, Teide
items.tip: Moderate | Volcanic rock formations, Teide views. One of the best accessible Teide walks. More manageable than hiking toward the summit and very scenic.

items.name: Teide National Park short trails
items.distance: 60-75 km depending on route, car or organised tour
items.tip: Easy to hard | Volcano landscapes, viewpoints, full day trip. The best full day hiking excursion on the island. Mount Teide is Spain's highest peak at 3,718 m and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

items.name: Local cafés and small bars in Valleseco
items.tip: Quick coffee, simple local food, closest option. Best for convenience rather than destination dining. Handy when you want something very close.

items.name: Santa Cruz city centre brunch and coffee spots
items.distance: 3.5-5 km, car, taxi, bus, or walk
items.tip: Brunch, coffee, casual daytime food. A useful option for something easy before sightseeing. The city scene has plenty of brunch and coffee spots alongside tapas and seafood.

items.name: La Noria street restaurants and bars
items.distance: 4-5 km, car, taxi, bus, or walk
items.tip: Evening drinks, tapas, lively atmosphere. A great area for dinner and drinks in central Santa Cruz. Best when you want a city evening rather than a beach meal.

items.name: El Porrón Tasca Andaluza
items.tip: Tapas, casual dinner, La Noria area. Listed among the best restaurants around La Noria, with strong ratings.

items.name: Bar Concepción
items.distance: 4-5 km, car, taxi, bus, or walk
items.tip: Wine, sharing plates, old town atmosphere. Lovely for a relaxed evening near the church and old streets, with outdoor seating, wine, and sharing plates.

items.name: Mercado Nuestra Señora de África
items.tip: Market breakfast, snacks, local produce, casual food. One of the best food culture stops in Santa Cruz, with fresh produce stalls and low-key bar restaurants.

items.name: San Sebastián 57
items.tip: Updated Canarian cuisine, nicer dinner. A stronger restaurant choice near the market, described by Michelin as an unmissable address with updated traditional cuisine.

items.name: Restaurante Origen
items.tip: Japanese and Peruvian, special dinner. A more polished option near the market, among the highly rated restaurants close to the Mercado.

items.name: El Rincón de Guadalupe
items.tip: Tacos, cocktails, casual dinner. Good if you want something different from Canarian food, among the top spots near the Mercado and La Noria.

items.name: San Andrés seafood restaurants
items.tip: Fresh fish, casual local lunch, Las Teresitas combo. One of the best nearby food areas. Great after Las Teresitas beach or before driving into Anaga.

items.name: Restaurante Rincón del Pescador, San Andrés
items.tip: Fish of the day, seafood, local lunch. A strong seafood option near Las Teresitas, often praised for its fish of the day and octopus.

items.name: La Pandorga, San Andrés
items.tip: Seafood, Mediterranean food, beach day lunch. A reliable pick near Las Teresitas, among the most popular San Andrés restaurants.

items.name: Tasquita Lucas, San Andrés
items.tip: Casual Spanish food, seafood, good value. A good smaller local option, highly rated among the cheaper eats near Las Teresitas.

items.name: Los Pinchitos, San Andrés
items.tip: Casual seafood, simple local meal. A practical, casual option near Las Teresitas, rated 8.5/10 for food, service, and atmosphere.

items.name: La Laguna cafés and restaurants
items.distance: 13-16 km, car, taxi, tram, or bus
items.tip: Historic town lunch, cafes, dinner. The best cultural food excursion from Santa Cruz. Great to combine with the UNESCO historic centre and a relaxed walk.

items.name: Guachinches near La Laguna and Tacoronte
items.tip: Local Canarian food, wine, rustic experience. Great for adventurous food guests. Guachinches are frills-free, budget-friendly spots serving simple home-style meals, mostly in the north and often seasonal.

items.name: Bajamar and Punta del Hidalgo restaurants
items.tip: Ocean views, casual lunch, natural pools day. A good food area if you visit the natural pools. Works well as a relaxed north coast day trip.

items.name: Taganana and Roque de las Bodegas restaurants
items.tip: Scenic lunch, fish, Anaga day trip. The best food stop during an Anaga excursion. Recommended for lunch after Taganana, Almáciga, or Benijo.

items.name: Local minimarkets and small shops in Valleseco
items.tip: Basics, drinks, snacks, quick essentials. Best for convenience. Use this for water, small groceries, and last-minute things rather than proper shopping.

items.name: Calle del Castillo
items.distance: 3.5-4.5 km, bus, taxi, car, or walk
items.tip: Fashion, shoes, cosmetics, city shopping. The main shopping street in Santa Cruz, running through the centre with international franchises, fashion shops, boutiques, cafes, and side streets worth exploring.

items.name: Plaza de España & Plaza de la Candelaria area
items.distance: 3.5-4.5 km, bus, taxi, car, or walk
items.tip: City centre shopping, cafes, sightseeing combo. A good starting point for a relaxed shopping walk, leading into Calle del Castillo, Imeldo Serís, and Valentín Sanz.

items.name: Calle del Pilar and side streets
items.distance: 3.5-4.5 km, bus, taxi, car, or walk
items.tip: Boutiques, local shops, cafes. Better if you want to wander beyond the main high street. The streets around Calle Castillo and Plaza del Príncipe mix shopping and food stops nicely.

items.name: Mercado Nuestra Señora de África
items.tip: Fresh produce, local food, flowers, small gifts. The best local market in Santa Cruz, known for fresh fruit, veg, fish, meat, and flowers. The 1944 building has lovely Moorish-style arches and patios.

items.name: Nivaria Center
items.tip: Comfortable shopping, cafes, easy parking. A polished shopping centre in central Santa Cruz, a handy reference point with parking and regular events.

items.name: Centro Comercial Meridiano
items.tip: Big mall, Primark, cinema, food court. The best mall in Santa Cruz, with nearly 80 stores, 15 cinema screens, a rooftop food court, and the only Primark on the island.

items.name: San Andrés small shops
items.tip: Beach day basics, snacks, simple errands. Useful before or after Las Teresitas. Not a shopping destination, but practical for drinks, beach snacks, or small necessities.

items.name: Centro Comercial Añaza Carrefour
items.tip: Hypermarket, large shopping, practical errands. Best for big grocery shopping or practical errands by car, with 100+ shops, a large Carrefour, and over 2,000 free parking spaces.

items.name: La Laguna Historic Centre shops
items.distance: 13-16 km, car, taxi, tram, or bus
items.tip: Boutiques, gifts, cafes, historic streets. The best cultural shopping excursion. La Laguna is a UNESCO World Heritage city, great for combining shopping, coffee, and a historic walk.

items.name: Mercado de La Laguna
items.distance: 13-16 km, car, taxi, tram, or bus
items.tip: Fresh produce, flowers, local food. A good local market in the historic centre, known for fresh products, flowers, and local gastronomy.

items.name: Local product shopping near Tacoronte
items.tip: Local wine, cheese, Canarian products. Good if you want local food products rather than normal retail. Combine with a guachinche lunch or a north coast drive.

items.name: Puerto de la Cruz shopping streets
items.tip: Day trip shopping, souvenirs, cafes. Nice if you are already visiting Puerto de la Cruz, Lago Martiánez, or La Orotava. Worth it as part of a full north coast day rather than only for shopping.

items.name: La Orotava local shops and crafts
items.tip: Traditional town, crafts, gifts. Better for atmosphere and local gifts than regular retail. Works well with Puerto de la Cruz or El Bollullo beach.

eatAndDrink.name: Karlina Restaurant
eatAndDrink.tip: Easiest no-car dinner option near Avlida. Ranked among the top restaurants near Tombs of the Kings.

eatAndDrink.name: Dias Zeus Greek Restaurant
eatAndDrink.tip: Reliable nearby option on Tombs of the Kings Avenue. Greek and Mediterranean with strong guest ratings.

eatAndDrink.name: Tea for Two
eatAndDrink.tip: Good for an easy breakfast or lunch close to home. Friendly service and great value.

eatAndDrink.name: Psarou Fish Tavern
eatAndDrink.tip: Cozy tavern known for fresh fish, traditional meze, and a friendly local vibe.

eatAndDrink.name: Kingfisher Taverna
eatAndDrink.tip: Small, relaxed taverna serving high-quality fish dishes and local favourites in a more upscale setting.

eatAndDrink.name: Three Little Pigs
eatAndDrink.tip: Local favourite with high-quality food, great service, and a lovely atmosphere. Their house wine is a treat!

eatAndDrink.name: Kings Avenue Mall Food Court
eatAndDrink.tip: Not special, but very practical. Good for families, quick food, coffee, or escaping the heat.

eatAndDrink.name: Old Town Paphos cafés
eatAndDrink.tip: More local feeling than the harbour. Nice for brunch, coffee, or early evening drinks.

eatAndDrink.name: Pallia Ilektriki
eatAndDrink.tip: A good Old Town option. Ranked among popular places near Old Town Paphos.

eatAndDrink.name: Ta Pinakia
eatAndDrink.tip: Good if you want something less harbour-focused. Well-rated near Old Town Paphos.

eatAndDrink.name: O Firis
eatAndDrink.tip: Modern twist on Cypriot cuisine with generous portions. Live music almost every evening. Book ahead!

eatAndDrink.name: Paphos Harbour restaurants
eatAndDrink.tip: Touristy but reliable. Many options for dinner, drinks, and a sunset walk by the sea.

eatAndDrink.name: King's Restaurant
eatAndDrink.tip: Popular harbour area choice. Good fish and Cypriot food.

eatAndDrink.name: Theo's Restaurant
eatAndDrink.tip: Good for seafood near the harbour. Sea bass, octopus, prawns, and calamari.

eatAndDrink.name: The Moorings Café Restaurant
eatAndDrink.tip: More about the harbour atmosphere than fine dining. Easy and relaxed.

eatAndDrink.name: West End Hotel & Restaurant
eatAndDrink.tip: Family-run with generous portions and delicious seafood, steps from the sea.

eatAndDrink.name: Oniro by the Sea
eatAndDrink.tip: One of the best scenic spots in the Paphos area. Combine with Sea Caves and Edro III shipwreck.

eatAndDrink.name: Kathikas wine village restaurants
eatAndDrink.tip: More authentic than the seafront. Best for a longer lunch or early dinner with wine tasting.

eatAndDrink.name: Latchi Harbour tavernas
eatAndDrink.tip: Best combined with a Blue Lagoon boat trip, Baths of Aphrodite, or a Polis day trip.

eatAndDrink.name: Lidl
eatAndDrink.tip: Budget-friendly with a wide range of groceries. Great for stocking up on basics.

eatAndDrink.name: AlphaMega
eatAndDrink.tip: Large supermarket with a wide variety of fresh produce and local Cypriot items.

shopping.name: Kings Avenue Mall
shopping.tip: The main shopping mall in Paphos. Wide range of shops, restaurants and a cinema.

shopping.name: Paphos Market
shopping.tip: Covered marketplace with stalls for clothing, bags, jewelry, beach gear and local souvenirs.

shopping.name: Old Town
shopping.tip: Wander through the beautiful Old Town and discover many small boutique stores and artisan shops.

gettingAround.name: Bus
gettingAround.info: To explore Tenerife by public transport, start by checking the TITSA website or the TITSA app. From Valleseco, the easiest option is usually to travel first to Intercambiador Santa Cruz, the main bus station in Santa Cruz. From there, you can walk into the city centre, take the tram to La Laguna, or change to buses going to other towns, airports, beaches, and the south of the island. For Playa de Las Teresitas, take TITSA line 910 towards San Andrés / Playa de Las Teresitas. You can either catch the bus from the Valleseco area when available, or first go to Intercambiador Santa Cruz and change there. Before leaving, I recommend checking the exact route on Google Maps, TITSA, or the TITSA app, as bus times can change depending on the day, season, and public holidays.

contacts.name: Emergency Services
contacts.note: Police, Ambulance, Fire. Free from any phone

contacts.name: Hospital Universitario Nuestra Señora de Candelaria
contacts.note: Main hospital in Santa Cruz de Tenerife

emergency.nearestHospital: Hospital Universitario Nuestra Señora de Candelaria, Ctra. Gral. del Rosario 145, Santa Cruz de Tenerife.
emergency.nearestPharmacy: Several pharmacies within walking distance on the main road. ask me if you need a recommendation.

steps: If you could give any dishes a quick wash and leave them on the drying rack, that would be great!
If you could clear out the fridge and pop any leftover food in the bin, I'd appreciate it!
If you could take the rubbish bags to the bins in the parking area — on your right as you walk out past the pool — that would be a big help!
Before heading off, please lock all windows, balcony doors and shutters.
Please switch off all the lights, the TV, and any electric appliances (oven, stove, blender) when you're done.
When you're ready to go, please lock up and return all keys to the keybox, resetting the code after.
Drop me a WhatsApp to let me know you've left — and I'd truly love a review if you enjoyed your stay!
checkout.lateCheckout: I try to be flexible wherever I can. If you'd like a late checkout, please let me know 1-2 days before your departure and I'll do my best to make it happen.
